input_candidates.truth_value: Figure 2(a) indicates specific cold-workability values for quaternary alloys: Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C (γ) hardness 466 HV, W=74%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.2C (γ+α) 302 HV, W>90%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.0C (γ) 295 HV, W>90%; Fe-20Mn-10Al-0.5C (γ+α) 270 HV, W=88%; Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C (γ+κ) 413 HV, W=0%; etc.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Cold-workability parameter W was defined as W = (t0 - tmin)/t0 × 100%, where t0 is initial thickness and tmin is minimum thickness before crack appearance during room-temperature cold-rolling.

input_candidates.truth_value: Fe-20Mn-11Al-1.8C-5Cr γ alloy after 1100°C WQ showed specific strength of 183 MPa·cm3/g with elongation 29.0%, and after 30% cold rolling specific strength over 230 MPa·cm3/g.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Fig. 8 presents a comparative plot of specific strength versus tensile elongation for the present alloys against conventional austenitic, ferritic, martensitic, precipitation hardened, dual-phase, super toughness and Fe-Mn-Si-Al TWIP steels.

input_candidates.truth_value: Cold-rolled Fe-20Mn-10Al-1.5C (93% reduction) achieved hardness 681 HV, yield strength 1970 MPa, UTS 2111 MPa, specific strength 323 MPa·cm3/g.
